theory.canonical_statement:
Notation / reminders (short): - \(g_{\mu
u}\) metric, \(g^{\mu
u}\) its inverse. Signature \((-+++)\). - Field: \(F_{\mu
u}=\partial_\mu A_
u-\partial_
u A_\mu\). - Scalar invariant: \(I\equiv F^{\alpha eta}F_{\alpha eta}\). - Covariant EM stress-energy: \[ T^{\mu
u}_{m EM}=F^{\mu\alpha}{F^
u}_{\!\alpha}-	frac14 g^{\mu
u}I. \] - Matter piece (generic imperfect fluid / gyro): \[ T^{\mu
u}_{m matter}=ho u^\mu u^
u + p\,h^{\mu
u} + q^\mu u^
u + q^
u u^\mu + \pi^{\mu
u}, \] with \(h^{\mu
u}=g^{\mu
u}+u^\mu u^
u\), \(u_\mu q^\mu=0\), \(u_\mu\pi^{\mu
u}=0\), \(\pi^\mu{}_\mu=0\). - Full tensor \(T^{\mu
u}=T^{\mu
u}_{m EM}+T^{\mu
u}_{m matter}\).
theory.technical_interpretation: Full Explicit Expansions — Both Observers: u_μ q^μ is an algebraic definition, written u_μ q^μ=0. It is an algebraic definition expressing the quantity directly in terms of the variables on its right-hand side. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
